title: Snap shares rise on fourth-quarter earnings that beat on sales
text:
Snap shares rise on fourth-quarter earnings that beat on sales

2025 Quarterly Revenue Trends

Q1 2025 revenue increased 14% year-over-year to $1,363 million, which was the strongest quarterly growth rate. Q2 2025 revenue was $1,345 million, an increase of 9% year-over-year. Q3 2025 revenue increased 10% year-over-year to $1,507 million, and Q4 2025 revenue increased 10% year-over-year to $1,716 million.

Full Year 2025 Performance

For the full year 2025, Snap achieved revenue of $5.93 billion, up 11% from $5.36 billion in 2024. The company showed significant profitability improvements: The company reduced its net loss to $460 million from $698 million in the prior year, while adjusted EBITDA increased to $689 million from $509 million.

Key Financial Metrics

Fourth quarter gross margin of 59%, up 4 percentage points sequentially and 2 percentage points year-over-year, with fourth quarter operating cash flow of $270 million and Free Cash Flow of $206 million.

Strategic Progress

CEO Evan Spiegel noted that Q4 results began to reflect the impact of our strategic pivot toward profitable growth, translating into revenue diversification and meaningful margin expansion. Full year adjusted EBITDA was $689 million, representing adjusted EBITDA flow-through of 32% in 2025.

User and Advertiser Growth

Total active advertisers increased 28% year-over-year in Q4, with strong performance in advertising optimization tools. Snap's monthly active user base grew to 946 million, an increase of 51 million or 6% year-over-year.
